claim: Human-in-the-loop approval gates should intercept tool calls without the agent knowing, preventing accidental execution while keeping prompts clean
actionable: Add a human review node AFTER your agent in n8n that intercepts tool outputs before returning; don't mention the review layer in system prompts to avoid agent hesitation
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: Use simple memory with a context window size (default 5) rather than external databases for single-agent chat workflows; increase to 50+ messages for longer conversations
actionable: In n8n AI agent memory settings, set context window to 50+ for production, not the default of 5, and pay attention to token costs from retained messages
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: Set response mode to 'using response nodes' in chat triggers (not 'streaming') when using human review or conditional flows; always include a final chat node to send output back
actionable: In chat trigger options, switch to 'using response nodes' mode, then add a chat node after your agent to route output—streaming mode won't work with response-node gates
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: Create tool descriptions in the format 'node name = tool name, node description = tool description' because LLMs receive these fields as the only definition of available tools
actionable: Rename n8n nodes to match their purpose and add detailed, complete descriptions; avoid relying on system prompt to compensate for unclear tool names/descriptions
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: Use field-level parameter descriptions to override AI behavior on specific fields; e.g., change 'summary' to 'title' to guide the model, especially on confusing API field names
actionable: In tool configurations, add descriptions to individual fields and rename parameter keys to clarify their purpose before letting the AI auto-fill them
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: LLMs typically don't know the current time; use the 'now' convenience function in expressions (e.g., in search fields) to inject the current date for reliable time-based queries
actionable: When building time-based AI workflows, use the 'now' function in n8n expressions for date/time defaults rather than relying on the model to know the current date
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: When an AI tool can only set specific fields (not all), it prevents misuse; design tools to expose only safe parameters to the agent while handling sensitive ones statically
actionable: In n8n tool configurations, use 'Let AI set' only on safe, non-critical fields; hardcode or template sensitive fields like 'to', 'from', or approval status to prevent agent drift
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: Test destructive actions in workflows using human review; copy the full execution data and re-run to iterate on formatting/logic without side effects
actionable: For calendar/email workflows, always test with dummy data first; use n8n's 'Copy to editor' on execution logs to re-test with real outputs without sending duplicate emails
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: Use Luxon date formatting (e.g., 'ddd, MMMM d, yyyy, t') inside expressions to make approval messages human-readable instead of UTC timestamps
actionable: Format dates in n8n human-review messages with Luxon syntax (available via dot notation) to show friendly dates instead of raw timestamps
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: For team workflows, clone the workflow for feature development instead of using branches (enterprise git support exists but not for standard plans); merge changes manually after testing
actionable: On n8n standard/pro, don't try multi-developer editing; copy the workflow, make changes, test in isolation, then manually update the production workflow
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: If workflow execution is stuck waiting for human input, set an automatic deny timeout (e.g., 10 minutes) to prevent indefinite accumulation of waiting executions
actionable: Enable wait time limits on human-review nodes to auto-deny after N minutes if no response, preventing resource exhaustion from ignored approvals
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: For larger automation systems, use sub-agents (specialized agents for sub-workflows) instead of adding all tools to one agent to reduce context bloat and allow model switching per domain
actionable: Break complex workflows into sub-agents: one for email, one for GitHub, one for Jira; call them from a router agent to keep each focused and swappable
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: Expose workflows as REST APIs with custom paths (e.g., POST /users) using webhook triggers; include full documentation/Swagger for external integrations
actionable: Add a webhook trigger to n8n workflows to make them callable via REST API; document the endpoint and payload schema so other teams can integrate
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle

claim: N8N's MCP server allows Claude/agents to discover and execute published workflows with control; enable per-workflow via settings to prevent accidental mutation of wrong workflows
actionable: Enable n8n MCP in instance settings and mark workflows as 'available in MCP' individually so external agents can safely call them without risk
source_title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation with n8n — Liam McGarrigle
